mainmatter.p: Let \(E\) be an object in a category \(\mathcal{C}\) such that all binary cartesian products with \(E\) exist. We say that \(E\) is exponentiable when the product endofunctor \(- \times E\) on \(\mathcal{C}\) has a right adjoint, which we shall write \(E \Rightarrow-\).
A carrable map \({E} \xrightarrow{p} {B}\) in a category \(\mathcal{C}\) is said to be exponentiable when it is exponentiable when viewed as an object of the slice category \({\mathcal{C}} _ {/ B}\).
